lesson.menu_title: The Sales Pitch -- Persuasive Language & Benefits
lesson.subtitle: Aula 19 -- The Sales Pitch -- Persuasive Language & Benefits
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rafael de Andrade Brandão | Aula 19 -- The Sales Pitch

listenings.text: Hi Rafael, this is Diana. Big news. Next week you will pitch our new service to an important client, and I want it to be a strong sales pitch. Let me share a few tips, so you persuade them with confidence. First, do not just list the features. A feature is what the product is. The benefit is what the client gets. So do not only say, our service is fast. Say, our service is fast, and that is why it will save you two hours a day. Always link the feature to the benefit. Second, sell the value, not the price. Show the client the worth of your offer. You can say, this is not a cost, it is an investment. Third, use strong, positive language to convince them. Say things like, not only is it reliable, but it is also easy to use. And remember the magic word: you. Talk about what the client gets, not about your company. Finally, back up your pitch. We offer a full guarantee, so there is no risk for them. If you connect every feature to a clear benefit, sell the value, and speak with confidence, you will win the deal. You have all the skills. Now go and pitch. Good luck, Rafael.

Okay, let me practice my sales pitch out loud before the meeting with the client. Good afternoon, and thank you for your time. Today I want to show you why our new service is the right choice for your team. Let me start with the value. This is not just a cost, it is an investment that pays for itself in three months. Now, here are the benefits. First, our service is fast. Not only is it fast, but it is also simple, and that is why it will save your team two hours every day. Second, it is reliable. We have a strong track record, so you can trust it from day one. And third, we offer a full guarantee. If you are not happy, you get your money back, so there is no risk for you. I am sure this offer is worth it. So, let me ask you, are you ready to win back two hours a day? I would love to convince you to start a trial. Thank you. Okay, that felt strong. I linked each feature to a benefit, I sold the value, and I asked for the next step. Diana will be proud.

extra_audio.text: Rafael gives a sales pitch to an important client. First, he thanks the client and says why he is there. Then he sells the value and explains that the service is an investment, not just a cost. Next, he lists the benefits and links each feature to what the client gets. After that, he backs up the pitch with a full guarantee, so there is no risk. Finally, he asks the client to start a trial and win the deal.
